title: Kotlin Abstract Classes
content:
### Abstract Classes in Kotlin

Abstract classes cannot be instantiated directly. They can contain both abstract (unimplemented) and concrete (implemented) members.

---

### Declaring Abstract Classes

```kotlin
abstract class Shape {
    abstract fun area(): Double  // No implementation
    
    fun description(): String {  // Concrete method
        return "Shape with area ${area()}"
    }
}
```

---

### Abstract Properties

```kotlin
abstract class Shape {
    abstract val name: String
    abstract fun area(): Double
}

class Circle(val radius: Double) : Shape() {
    override val name = "Circle"
    override fun area() = Math.PI * radius * radius
}
```

---

### Abstract vs Open

| Feature | Abstract | Open |
|---------|----------|------|
| Instantiation | Cannot be instantiated | Can be instantiated |
| `abstract` members | Must be overridden | May be overridden |
| `open` keyword | Implicitly open | Must be explicit |
| Constructor | Can have constructors | Can have constructors |

---

### Abstract Class with Constructor

```kotlin
abstract class Vehicle(val brand: String, val model: String) {
    abstract fun maxSpeed(): Int
    
    fun start() {
        println("$brand $model is starting")
    }
}

class Car(brand: String, model: String) : Vehicle(brand, model) {
    override fun maxSpeed() = 200
}
```

---

### Difference from Interfaces

Abstract classes can have state (constructor parameters, properties with backing fields) while interfaces cannot.

```kotlin
abstract class AbstractRepo {
    protected val cache = mutableMapOf<String, Any>()  // State allowed
    abstract fun fetch(id: String): Any?
}

interface Repo {
    fun fetch(id: String): Any?  // No state allowed
}
```

---

### When to Use Abstract Classes

Use abstract classes when:
- Classes share a common state (constructor parameters)
- There is a clear "is-a" relationship
- You need protected members accessible to subclasses

> **Key Point:** Abstract classes are implicitly open. They provide a common base with shared implementation while forcing subclasses to implement specific behavior.

quiz.question: Can abstract classes be instantiated?
options: Only if they have no abstract methods

quiz.question: Can abstract classes have constructor parameters?
